108378.explanation: Management requires (too) much knowledge and skills to be left entirely to volunteers, as has become apparent in several foundations and associations in recent years.

108381.explanation: From the framework of promoting sustainability and entrepreneurship.

108384.explanation: These employers do have a responsibility for housing, but not on their own premises.

108386.explanation: We must focus on education and raising awareness about the disadvantages of burning.

108388.explanation: As an alternative to consumer fireworks it's fine, as long as Sunday is not involved.

108392.explanation: You can also do sports without membership of a club, think of running. Furthermore, there are various 'funds', such as special assistance etc.

108394.explanation: There is a policy for refurbishing school buildings: the IHP. Let's proceed with that.

108396.explanation: Sports clubs must stand on their own feet.

108397.explanation: Do not compromise on our own cultural-historical heritage.

108398.explanation: The current number of enforcement officers (boa's) is sufficient.

108399.explanation: The sense of security must be strengthened.

108401.explanation: On the one hand agree, starters have it difficult. On the other hand disagree, municipalities already have many expenses and need to cut back.

108404.explanation: The SGP wants to prevent cluttering and damage to natural values.

108405.explanation: The SGP wants to transform the Vinkenwaard-North industrial area into a residential area.

108406.explanation: If possible, yes, but this will not be feasible for every welfare recipient.

108408.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of a generic increase of this income limit. However, it does support a good safety net for people who get into (or threaten to get into) financial problems.

108410.explanation: In the context of the energy transition, the SGP wants to stimulate the use of electric cars. This includes sufficient charging points in the neighborhood.

108412.explanation: The SGP always advocates identity-related care.

108414.explanation: Down to the average Coelo score.

108417.explanation: A collective day of rest is beneficial for the entire society.

108418.explanation: There must be a good balance in providing housing to young starters from Alblasserdam and status holders.

108423.explanation: With a relatively high number of social rental homes, you price yourself out of the market as a municipality. 30 percent is the national guideline.

108425.explanation: In the context of the housing shortage.

108427.explanation: There must be extra surveillance in places known for trouble.

108428.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest; that is beneficial for everyone.

108429.explanation: Soon the new transferium will open (located under the Noordbrug). We see this as the main boarding place for the shuttle to Kinderdijk.

108430.explanation: This is a task for the province. However, we are positive about the statement because Alblasserdam depends on bus and Waterbus public transport.

108378.explanation: We find that too vulnerable. If that person withdraws, there is a risk to continuity, causing the municipality to intervene. Volunteer boards can never bear responsibility if financial setbacks occur. Therefore, cooperation with the municipality is important.

108380.explanation: It must be possible as long as one complies with the applicable laws and regulations.

108381.explanation: Because of this, we expect sustainability to be achieved faster. (Vulnerable) neighborhoods with insufficient financial means should receive earlier and more support, according to established criteria.

108384.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers the deployment of seasonal workers in this sector important. Because they now find it difficult to temporarily live near their work, we believe the employer should facilitate this.

108386.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves is harmful to health (fine particulate matter) and bad for the environment.

108388.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA sees this as an alternative to the fireworks ban.

108392.explanation: We want to remove barriers for children from vulnerable families to join sports clubs. Lack of money should never be an argument to not play sports.

108394.explanation: Good education deserves an attractive facility, starting with the new construction of 't Nokkenwiel.

108396.explanation: Sports clubs are an important part (and backbone) of our society.

108397.explanation: As long as there is financial room for this, we are not in favor.

108398.explanation: Partly thanks to our contribution to an amendment adopted council-wide, the current number of enforcement officers is sufficient.

108399.explanation: Once at locations in the nightlife where the risk of violence is greatest. Especially in the hospitality area around Dam Square and nearby.

108401.explanation: We want to finance dozens of homes from the available Eneco funds.

108402.explanation: Residents of Alblasserdam should be given priority to continue living in this village.

108404.explanation: We should grant this if applicable.

108405.explanation: There must be houses in this region and we need to cooperate with other municipalities for this, such as Molenlanden (Sportpark Souburgh).

108406.explanation: We must consider personal circumstances and possibilities. Also take into account retraining and the desire to continue learning.

108410.explanation: Expansion is important in the context of further sustainability.

108411.explanation: For traffic safety, cleaner air, less energy use, and less frequent replacement of asphalt.

108412.explanation: We should carefully look at the costs of maintaining all this.

108414.explanation: Only if the social domain comes under further financial pressure.

108417.explanation: For us, Sunday is a day when institutions and stores may open, but only after a good conversation with the community has taken place first.

108418.explanation: We adhere to national policy and will act based on solidarity.

108423.explanation: This is included in our election program. These homes are necessary to provide good housing for a large part of our residents.

108425.explanation: We are in favor of sharing homes. Residents of larger homes should be guided to make this possible, for example with a relocation agent and an associated compensation.

108427.explanation: This area needs deployment of community enforcement officers (boa's). Nuisance in the nature and recreation area must be addressed.

108428.explanation: Residents themselves should be given space to organize events on Sundays.

108429.explanation: We must continue to encourage tourists to use the parking facilities at the transferium.

108430.explanation: Water transport is important to us. The province is responsible for its operation. The municipality must invest in the water bus if the province fails to do so.

108378.explanation: Initiatives and residents who want to take responsibility must be encouraged by the municipality. This requires clear agreements.

108380.explanation: We welcome initiatives and entrepreneurship at the VVD.

108381.explanation: There are national subsidies for that. It is not a municipal task and there is no budget for it here.

108384.explanation: We do not (yet) have that. If it becomes relevant, it should be preferred over housing in regular homes.

108386.explanation: We are against patronizing and banning, but in favor of informing and discouraging to promote more conscious heating and cleaner air.

108388.explanation: Facilitating and supporting initiatives is possible, but organizing them ourselves is not a municipal task.

108392.explanation: We already have various facilities and arrangements for people on social assistance.

108394.explanation: We still have some relatively old school buildings that really need investment. This is a municipal task and we must take this responsibility. After all, it concerns the future of the next generation.

108396.explanation: Associations should primarily take responsibility themselves. The municipality can help but is not financially responsible for associations.

108397.explanation: It is important to be careful with what is valuable and should be preserved. Restraint is needed in applying the monument label to locations. Owners may also contribute more themselves.

108398.explanation: Safety is a core task of the (local) government. We must invest in that, especially considering the incidents, vandalism, and nuisance experienced by residents.

108399.explanation: Where it helps countering nuisance and removes the feeling of insecurity among residents, we should focus on that with (mobile) cameras.

108401.explanation: Facilitating our young people from Alblasserdam on the housing market is an important matter and we want to stimulate this with a starter loan.

108402.explanation: It is important that local residents get priority and can settle within their own municipality where they want.

108404.explanation: Although we do not have large holiday parks, this helps combat the housing shortage.

108405.explanation: Village boundaries are not the standard. The demand for homes is large. We want to preserve greenery but are open to ideas that can be integrated. For example, at Vinkenwaard-North, where homes can be built if businesses relocate to the Nedstaal site.

108406.explanation: People receiving benefits must remain actively involved in society, both for their own well-being and to increase their chances of employment.

108408.explanation: This is a national scheme. Locally we have sufficient safety nets for those who need it.

108410.explanation: We currently have quite a few charging points. More spots exclusively for electric vehicles increase parking pressure, which we do not want.

108411.explanation: The road network layout should be designed for traffic flow and safety. Simply lowering the speed helps no one.

108412.explanation: It is important that people receive the care they need. People have freedom of choice, but that does not mean an option must be offered for every identity.

108414.explanation: By building more homes (area expansion), the property tax (ozb) can be lowered.

108417.explanation: It is up to the entrepreneurs themselves to make that choice. This can be done perfectly with respect for the Christian norms and values of a large part of our residents.

108418.explanation: We must adhere to the national agreements, but beyond that we would give priority to our own residents.

108423.explanation: With 30 percent, we already exceed the national standard. A village like ours benefits from a diverse housing supply.

108425.explanation: On a limited scale and under good conditions, this should be possible.

108427.explanation: In places where nuisance and vandalism are experienced, active efforts can be expected from the municipality.

108428.explanation: We assess every event application primarily on content and not solely on the day of the week on which it takes place.

108429.explanation: We do not see the added value, but we are open to being convinced.

108430.explanation: We benefit from good public transport, but have no money to contribute to public transport. That is also not a municipal responsibility.

108378.explanation: The CDA supports volunteer boards provided that good agreements are made between the municipality and the boards about responsibilities, risk management, financial and personnel support. There must be a fixed point of contact for these boards within the municipality.

108380.explanation: The CDA believes that farmers should be given a clear perspective in the transition to less environmentally polluting operations. We support initiatives such as shops or recreation around the farm or conversion to care centers.

108381.explanation: The CDA is cautious about financial support for residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy. The municipality should mainly act as a stimulator and facilitator, but not provide structural financial contributions. Initiatives must be self-sufficient and sustainable.

108384.explanation: The CDA agrees with this. Employers in the agriculture and horticulture sectors and the maritime sector are responsible for good housing for migrant laborers. By organizing this on their own premises, we prevent nuisance, companies fulfill their social role, and neighborhoods remain livable.

108386.explanation: The municipality should make filters mandatory. The housing market in Alblasserdam has too many old homes with wood stoves to immediately ban this entirely. Nuisance to others must be limited. Conscious wood burning should remain possible.

108388.explanation: The CDA follows the national fireworks ban. A municipal fireworks display on New Year's Eve is fine, but safety, the environment, and animal welfare come first. The municipality can better focus on safe and festive alternatives without nuisance.

108392.explanation: The municipality currently has sufficient schemes to assist with this. For youth growing up in poverty, the municipality must continue to pay membership fees via the SMS Children's Fund.

108394.explanation: Only for school buildings where this is necessary. Renovation or new construction of school buildings is done systematically based on the integrated housing plan.

108396.explanation: The CDA strongly supports financial assistance for associations if they (temporarily) need it to survive. Other non-financial support is also possible until an association can be as self-sufficient as possible again.

108397.explanation: Monument care is part of the cultural heritage of our village. The CDA would like to preserve and support that.

108398.explanation: The CDA supports additional deployment of municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) for safety in Alblasserdam. Enforcement contributes to livability and safety, especially in places with nuisance. We do not see enforcement as a solution but as the final step in a broader approach where prevention and assistance are more important.

108399.explanation: The CDA is positive about this in public spaces for the sake of safety. The goal and means must make a real contribution to safety and the perception of safety. We do not currently see this in our village.

108401.explanation: The CDA believes that housing construction should primarily be allocated to residents of the region. First purchase rights are part of this. We are not in favor of starter loans. This should be part of national policy.

108402.explanation: The CDA is positive about giving priority to local residents when allocating (social) rental homes. This keeps our social cohesion intact.

108404.explanation: In Alblasserdam this is of little relevance, but given the housing shortage, the CDA is basically positive about the use of holiday homes as permanent residences to help provide affordable housing for residents.

108405.explanation: The CDA supports the policy to limit new construction outside the village boundaries in accordance with regional agreements. At the same time, we are positive about building on the Souburg sports park, provided this is done in good consultation and with clear arrangements with the neighboring municipality.

108406.explanation: Doing socially meaningful work can help people become part of society again. However, this must be assessed on a case-by-case basis. The CDA is not in favor of imposing extra conditions for receiving social assistance benefits.

108408.explanation: The CDA favors raising the income limit for poverty assistance. This way, the working poor can receive help as well.

108410.explanation: The CDA supports the idea of more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars. This promotes sustainable transport and contributes to a future-proof Alblasserdam, while ensuring sufficient accessibility and convenience for residents and visitors.

108411.explanation: The CDA is cautious about 30 km/h zones within built-up areas. Long main routes in Alblasserdam are important for smooth traffic flow, with 50 km/h remaining. In residential neighborhoods, we support 30 km/h for safety, tranquility, and livability.

108412.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people who need care can receive it from a provider that fits their faith, culture, or belief system as much as possible.

108414.explanation: The CDA believes that the property tax (ozb) must remain affordable. Taxes should be used transparently, fairly, and purposefully, with attention to families, the elderly, and entrepreneurs, so that everyone contributes to a livable and financially healthy village. We follow the national average as much as possible.

108417.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of shopping on Sundays. Sunday rest fits the religious character of the village and respectful coexistence. This preserves peace and social cohesion, while shopkeepers retain their opportunities on other days.

108418.explanation: The CDA is in favor of national regulations on this subject.

108423.explanation: The CDA supports the goal of at least 32 percent social housing in Alblasserdam. This provides affordable homes for families and young people, strengthens social cohesion, and ensures a balanced, future-proof living environment in line with national shortages.

108425.explanation: The CDA is cautious about removing homes from the housing stock to rent them as non-independent living units. In certain neighborhoods, livability is already under pressure because of this.

108427.explanation: The CDA supports additional deployment of municipal enforcement officers at the Lammetjeswiel recreation lake due to increased nuisance and vandalism over the past year. Enforcement is necessary for safety and livability but is not a solution for deeper problems. Those require a broader approach.

108428.explanation: The CDA opts for a careful balance. Sunday rest deserves respect, but a strict cutoff at midnight is not always necessary. We are open to events with a limited extension into Sunday, while remaining cautious about events on Sunday itself.

108429.explanation: The CDA is positive about an extra stop in the village. This gives tourists the chance to make a stopover, which strengthens the local retail sector. If well organized, this connects accessibility with livability and the local economy.

108430.explanation: The CDA is cautious about additional municipal investments in the Waterbus. Public transport should be viewed coherently. The province has a major responsibility in this. The municipality's role is mainly facilitating and stimulating, not structural financing.

108378.explanation: Managing a sports hall may be a bit much for most residents, but if a foundation is set up for management, that's fine! We fully support adopting small parks, but with clear guidelines! We want to green the municipality, so not adopt and then pave over.

108380.explanation: Recreation and relaxation may grow in Alblasserdam. More recreation and relaxation around the local village is great.

108381.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs must definitely receive support from the municipality. This does not have to be financial. Financial contributions can be made through (existing) subsidy schemes, but this is not the basis of the support.

108384.explanation: Labor migrants must be decently housed. The employer has a great responsibility in this. This can be on their own premises, but that is not a requirement. If such a situation arises, it is good to create a policy for it. As far as we know, this (still) does not occur in our village.

108386.explanation: Education about burning remains important. Attention must also be paid to the effects on the environment and air quality. In case of repeated nuisance, enforcement must be possible so that neighborhoods remain livable.

108388.explanation: Given the financial situation, we are not in favor of a fireworks show organized by the municipality. If there are initiatives from society, this could be an option.

108392.explanation: The municipality can contribute to making sports possible, but not all memberships are the same. Various schemes are already available for this purpose.

108394.explanation: Together with all schools, it has been determined (in the Integral Housing Plan) when which school building will be renovated or replaced. D66 wants to adhere to this. We are therefore in favor of improvements, but according to the current schedule and not all school buildings in the coming period.

108396.explanation: Associations are important for the village. The municipality should support them, but this support does not always have to be financial. The best solution must be considered per situation. Associations also have their own responsibility here.

108397.explanation: We have few monuments in Alblasserdam. For the monuments that do exist, we share responsibility. When it concerns monuments in private ownership, this is primarily the responsibility of the owner.

108398.explanation: The budget cut on enforcement has been reversed, but investing more money in this means making painful choices, which will also come at the expense of prevention.

108399.explanation: Some privacy and restraint are very desirable. Where safety is at stake, a camera can always be used (temporarily).

108401.explanation: This requires a long-term commitment in terms of extra administrative and financial effort from the municipality. Moreover, this can also drive prices up further. Affordable construction must also remain possible in Alblasserdam.

108402.explanation: It would be nice if Alblasserdammers who want to can continue living in Alblasserdam. Unfortunately, there is a huge shortage of social rental homes. For D66, it does not feel right to exclude people from outside the municipality. We strive for the fairest possible allocation of houses.

108404.explanation: Not applicable for Alblasserdam, we have no holiday homes.

108405.explanation: This is actually not an issue. The only available space left is protected Natura 2000 area. Additionally, D66 is not in favor of housing projects in the Kortland polder.

108406.explanation: People on social assistance must, if possible, be helped out of it. Socially meaningful (volunteer) work helps them to get out faster.

108408.explanation: In recent years, this has been achieved for the municipal finances in Alblasserdam. Due to a major budget cut requirement, it was decided to revert to the regional average. We support this as long as the municipal finances do not allow otherwise, painful as it may be.

108410.explanation: Many places were designated in the past council period. Depending on developments, it must be seen if more places are needed. D66 supports the use of special tiles, allowing people without a driveway to safely charge their car in front of their home.

108411.explanation: Main roads must remain at 50 km/h for accessibility and emergency services. Residential access roads must be set to 30 km/h or designated as a residential area.

108412.explanation: For care provided by the municipality, we rely on the professionalism of care providers. Necessary care must be available to everyone. By having fewer providers, this remains affordable! If you consciously choose another care provider, this will be at your own expense.

108414.explanation: To keep finances healthy, it must be adjusted for inflation at a minimum. Costs for the municipality also rise due to inflation. If costs keep increasing and government contributions decrease, raising the property tax (ozb) becomes unavoidable. Only if we see no other choice!

108417.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the choice to be open on Sundays.

108418.explanation: As a municipality, we also have an obligation (the Distribution Act) to house a certain number of status holders annually. We must comply with this. However, they should be treated equally to other urgent housing seekers who also receive priority.

108423.explanation: In Alblasserdam we apply 30 percent. That is a good starting point.

108425.explanation: This creates clutter. Houses can still be split.

108427.explanation: Enforcement already frequently patrols along the Lammetjeswiel. More focus on that place would come at the expense of other priority areas.

108428.explanation: Many people value Sunday rest, others prefer Saturday or Friday. Some have no preference. If someone wants to hold an event on Sunday, it should be considered just like on any other day.

108429.explanation: There may still be some nice memories to be made in Alblasserdam. This way local entrepreneurs could generate extra income.

108430.explanation: The Waterbus is important for our village. Currently, Alblasserdam already contributes partly to the Waterbus costs via the Drechtsteden. An additional financial contribution is not feasible for a municipality like Alblasserdam. The province is responsible for extra funding.

108378.explanation: Can add value and reduce costs.

108380.explanation: We have three farmers. This depends on the circumstances.

108381.explanation: Facilitate and in special cases financially support, for example when forming a corporation.

108388.explanation: As a municipality, we do not want to finance and organize a fireworks show. If a private initiative arises from the village, we can assist through proper permits.

108392.explanation: We want to continue the current scheme as ChristenUnie. Those who cannot afford to play sports can apply via the Brughuis for a sports membership payment. For children: via the scheme with Stichting Leergeld or the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. For adults: via a scheme under the Sports Agreement.

108394.explanation: Already arranged in the Integral Housing Plan, a joint multi-year plan by municipalities and school boards to organize the future of school buildings, including new construction, renovation, and sustainability.

108396.explanation: Associations already receive subsidies; if necessary, they should raise membership fees.

108398.explanation: Therefore, the budget cut on security has been undone. Community enforcement officers (Boa's) are now being trained.

108399.explanation: We must detect more unwanted activities and charge for damages and the like.

108401.explanation: That would be a good arrangement.

108405.explanation: First utilize all possibilities within the municipal boundaries, such as Vinkenwaard-Noord.

108406.explanation: Only if this leads to a pathway out of social assistance.

108408.explanation: Residents currently receive a financial contribution called a Personal Minimum Budget if their income is up to 110 percent of the statutory social minimum. ChristenUnie wants to raise this percentage back to 120 percent so that the group of Alblasserdammers living just above the social assistance threshold can also benefit again. This aligns with the broader goal of reducing financial barriers for vulnerable households and strengthening their livelihood security.

108410.explanation: Alblasserdam has recently made quite a few possible. This prepares us well for the next ten years.

108412.explanation: Freedom of choice must be present.

108414.explanation: Only if there is no other way to keep facilities open.

108417.explanation: One day of rest per week is good for everyone.

108418.explanation: Priority for refugees should not mean that Alblasserdammers never get a turn again. There must be a weighted distribution, for example a maximum of 10 percent.

108423.explanation: The current rate of 30 percent is sufficient.

108425.explanation: If there is demand, this can be a partial solution for seasonal workers and those who want it. It can also have a positive effect on the urgent housing shortage.

108427.explanation: Nuisance and vandalism often occur at times when enforcement officers are not working. Deploying them then would cost a lot of extra money and probably yield little result. A good alternative would be camera surveillance, with ChristenUnie advocating the position that if the perpetrator is identified, he/she should cover the costs of the damages.

108428.explanation: Sunday rest is important for a large part of the Alblasserdammers.

108429.explanation: Fine if there is demand for it. Also good for local retailers.

108430.explanation: We already do that. A few neighboring municipalities could contribute more.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more events on Sundays.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet op zondag meer

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own business premises.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Alblasserdam.

titleParts.text: In Alblasserdam moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should deploy more enforcement officers (BOAs) around the Lammetjeswiel recreational lake.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.
A lake or pond where you are allowed to swim, windsurf and/or go boating.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: With money.

statements.title: Alblasserdam should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Alblasserdam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in the Waterbus.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: At least 35 percent of newly built homes should be social rental housing. Across the Netherlands that is currently 28 percent.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen moet minimaal 35 procent een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In heel Nederland is dat nu 28 procent.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The shuttle bus between the Marineweg parking area and Kinderdijk should get an extra stop at the village.
